methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Manifold Bio is a Series A-stage biotech company founded by Gleb Kuznetsov and Pierce Ogden that has raised $40M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in the Biotech & Health sector, including Colossal Biosciences, Abridge, and Chai Discovery.

Given data constraints, only the stage-progression method proved applicable. Using total capital raised ($40M) as a base proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $80M. The peer-median approach could not be executed due to insufficient datapoints within narrow filtering criteria (0 same-stage peers, 2 adjacent-stage peers, 0 similar-tier peers)—below the 3-point minimum threshold. Public-comp anchoring using the sector's 6.9x median P/S multiple across the 3 public tickers could not be applied absent the target's revenue disclosure, standard for private companies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $68M–$92M (low to high), with $80M as the point estimate. Key uncertainties center on the absence of direct peer valuation data and undisclosed revenue, which prevented multi-method triangulation. Any refinement would require disclosure of Manifold Bio's revenue or identification of closer-stage private comparables with published valuations.
